description: Curated top-journal literature database for medical ML prediction methodology. Only includes publications from high-impact journals (IF>10), authoritative guidelines (EQUATOR/Cochrane/FDA), or foundational statistical methods papers. Agents should query this database when evaluating methodology or justifying gate requirements.

categories.reporting_guideline: 报告指南 — TRIPOD+AI, STARD-AI, CONSORT-AI, SPIRIT-AI
categories.risk_of_bias: 偏倚风险评估 — PROBAST+AI, risk of bias tools
categories.sample_size: 样本量方法学 — Riley EPV, pmsampsize, minimum sample size
categories.leakage: 数据泄漏研究 — split contamination, label leakage, preprocessing leakage
categories.fairness: 公平性与公正性 — algorithmic fairness, equalized odds, disparate impact
categories.calibration: 校准与决策曲线 — ECE, DCA, NRI, IDI, DeLong
categories.validation: 外部验证方法学 — external validation, transport, generalization
categories.reproducibility: 可重复性与透明性 — SLSA, attestation, open science
categories.systematic_review: 系统评价方法学 — ML vs regression, data quality, meta-analysis

inclusion: Published in journal with IF > 10 OR domain-leading specialty journal
Authoritative guideline from EQUATOR Network, Cochrane, FDA, MHRA, EMA
exclusion: Preprints without peer review (exception: arXiv with subsequent journal acceptance)
Single-center validation studies without methodological contribution

entries.title: TRIPOD+AI statement: updated guidance for reporting clinical prediction models that use regression or machine learning methods
entries.authors: Collins GS, Moons KGM, Dhiman P, et al.
key_requirements: 27-item checklist supersedes TRIPOD 2015
Fairness assessment now explicitly recommended
Open science practices (code/data sharing) required
Model availability and reproducibility documentation
Harmonized terminology between ML and clinical research
Abstract checklist (TRIPOD+AI for Abstracts)

entries.title: PROBAST+AI: an updated quality, risk of bias, and applicability assessment tool for prediction models using regression or artificial intelligence methods
entries.authors: Moons KGM, Damen JAA, Kaul T, et al.
key_requirements: Two-part structure: model development (16 signalling questions) + model evaluation (18 signalling questions)
Four domains: participants/data sources, predictors, outcome, analysis
Applicability assessment across subpopulations
Aligned with TRIPOD+AI for consistent reporting
High interrater reliability demonstrated

entries.title: The STARD-AI reporting guideline for diagnostic accuracy studies using artificial intelligence
entries.authors: Sounderajah V, Guni A, Liu X, Collins GS, et al.
key_requirements: 18 new or modified items beyond STARD 2015
Dataset practices documentation
AI index test evaluation methodology
Algorithmic bias and fairness considerations
Applicability and generalizability assessment

entries.title: The TRIPOD-LLM reporting guideline for studies using large language models
entries.authors: Gallifant J, Afshar M, et al.
key_requirements: 19 main items and 50 subitems for LLM studies
Modular format for various LLM research designs
Living guideline with planned updates
Transparency and human oversight emphasis

entries.title: Minimum sample size for developing a multivariable prediction model: PART II - binary and time-to-event outcomes
entries.authors: Riley RD, Snell KIE, Ensor J, et al.
key_requirements: EPV >= 10 as minimum for binary outcomes
Target shrinkage factor >= 0.9 (max 10% overfitting)
Cox-Snell R² criterion for sample size adequacy
pmsampsize R package for calculation
Four criteria: small optimism, small absolute difference, precise overall risk, precise CI

entries.title: A general sample size framework for developing or updating a clinical prediction model
key_requirements: Simulation-based sample size framework for any ML method
Reference model approach for anticipated performance
Synthetic dataset for joint predictor distribution
Assurance-based targeting of predictive performance

entries.title: Larger sample sizes are needed when developing a clinical prediction model using machine learning in oncology: methodological systematic review
entries.authors: Tsegaye S, Snell KIE, Riley RD, et al.
key_requirements: Only 1/36 ML studies justified sample size
5/17 met minimum required sample size
ML models likely require larger sample than regression
Median deficit of 302 event participants

entries.title: Importance of sample size on the quality and utility of AI-based prediction models for healthcare
key_requirements: Most AI studies do not justify sample size
Inadequate datasets are common
Good Machine Learning Practice (FDA/MHRA/HC) principles apply
Sample size considerations critical for model reliability

entries.title: Data leakage inflates prediction performance in connectome-based machine learning models
key_requirements: Feature selection leakage and repeated subjects drastically inflate performance
Other leakage forms have more minor effects
Small samples amplify leakage effects
Must account for all leakage sources before claiming performance

entries.title: Diagnostic Codes in AI Prediction Models and Label Leakage of Same-Admission Clinical Outcomes
key_requirements: ICD diagnostic codes can leak same-admission outcomes
Label leakage inflates in-hospital mortality prediction performance
Temporal awareness needed for diagnosis code predictors
Codes assigned during encounter may encode the predicted outcome

entries.title: Leakage and the reproducibility crisis in machine-learning-based science
key_requirements: 294 studies across 17 fields affected by data leakage
Eight types of leakage errors classified
When corrected, ML models did not outperform older methods
Proposed model info sheets for prevention

entries.title: Data leakage in machine learning studies creep into meta-analytic estimates of predictive performance
key_requirements: High percentage of peer-reviewed studies have apparent data leakage
Biased studies inflate pooled meta-analytic performance estimates
REFORMS and PROBAST-AI quality assessment recommended
Careful exclusion of biased studies needed in meta-analyses

entries.title: Algorithm fairness in artificial intelligence for medicine and healthcare
entries.authors: Chen IY, Pierson E, Rose S, et al.
key_requirements: Biases from data acquisition, genetic variation, labelling variability
Inequalities in diagnosis, treatment, and billing
Mitigation via disentanglement, federated learning, explainability
Framework for assessing AI fairness in clinical workflows

entries.title: Generative models improve fairness of medical classifiers under distribution shifts
key_requirements: Diffusion models learn realistic augmentations for underrepresented groups
Synthetic samples improve fairness without sacrificing accuracy
Applicable across multiple medical disciplines
Label-efficient approach for fairness improvement

entries.title: Bias recognition and mitigation strategies in artificial intelligence healthcare applications
key_requirements: Demographic parity, equal opportunity, equalized odds metrics
Fairness-accuracy tradeoff is central challenge
Data collection must reflect population diversity
Special consideration for subgroup nuances

entries.title: A scoping review and evidence gap analysis of clinical AI fairness
key_requirements: Significant disconnects between technical solutions and clinical applications
Evidence gaps in clinical AI fairness implementation
Need for standardized fairness assessment frameworks

entries.title: Identifying and mitigating algorithmic bias in the safety net
key_requirements: Equal opportunity difference (EOD) threshold: absolute subgroup EOD <5%
Threshold adjustment and reject option classification for mitigation
Accuracy reduction must be <10% after mitigation
Alert rate change must be <20% after mitigation

entries.title: Reporting guidelines for clinical trial reports for interventions involving artificial intelligence: the CONSORT-AI extension
entries.authors: Liu X, Rivera SC, Moher D, et al.
key_requirements: Minimum reporting for AI clinical trial reports
Extension of CONSORT for AI interventions
Complements TRIPOD-ML and STARD-AI for non-trial studies

entries.title: Guidelines for clinical trial protocols for interventions involving artificial intelligence: the SPIRIT-AI extension
entries.authors: Rivera SC, Liu X, et al.
key_requirements: Protocol-level reporting for AI clinical trials
Pre-specification of AI intervention details
Complements CONSORT-AI

entries.title: Evaluating the added predictive ability of a new marker: from area under the ROC curve to reclassification and beyond
entries.authors: Pencina MJ, D'Agostino RB Sr, D'Agostino RB Jr, Vasan RS
key_requirements: Net Reclassification Improvement (NRI) definition
Integrated Discrimination Improvement (IDI) definition
Category-based and category-free (continuous) NRI
Standard method for comparing prediction models

entries.title: Comparing the areas under two or more correlated receiver operating characteristic curves: a nonparametric approach
entries.authors: DeLong ER, DeLong DM, Clarke-Pearson DL
key_requirements: Nonparametric test for comparing two correlated AUC values
Standard method for model comparison in medical prediction
Required by Nature Medicine, JAMA, Lancet for model comparisons

entries.title: Decision curve analysis: a novel method for evaluating prediction models
key_requirements: Net benefit calculation across threshold probabilities
Clinical utility assessment beyond discrimination
Standard for demonstrating model value in clinical practice
Required by BMJ, Lancet DH for prediction model studies

entries.title: SLSA Supply-chain Levels for Software Artifacts: Requirements v1.0
key_requirements: Build provenance and integrity verification
Multi-level attestation framework (L0-L4)
Platform-trust-boundary guarantees
Standardized attestation envelope format

entries.title: Beyond Comparing Machine Learning and Logistic Regression in Clinical Prediction Modelling: Shifting from Model Debate to Data Quality
key_requirements: ML performance gains in structured/tabular clinical data remain inconsistent
Data quality, not model complexity, drives clinical utility
No single algorithm excels across all metrics
Fairness, accuracy, generalizability, stability, parsimony must be balanced

entries.title: Deepchecks: Testing and Validating ML Models & Data
key_requirements: Automated leakage detection for tabular data
Train/test distribution drift checks
Feature drift monitoring

entries.title: Good Machine Learning Practice for Medical Device Development: Guiding Principles
key_requirements: 10 guiding principles for ML medical device development
Multi-disciplinary expertise required
Good software engineering and security practices
Representative data for intended patient population
Focus on model performance on clinically relevant subgroups

entries.title: Statistical primer: sample size considerations for developing and validating clinical prediction models
entries.authors: Martin GP, Riley RD, et al.
key_requirements: Nagelkerke R² of 0.15 as default if no prior information
Target shrinkage factor >= 0.9
ML algorithms likely require even larger samples than regression
Sample size applies to penalized regression and ML alike

entries.title: Algorithmic fairness and bias mitigation for clinical machine learning with deep reinforcement learning
key_requirements: Reinforcement learning for algorithmic bias mitigation
Demonstrated on COVID-19 screening and discharge prediction
Addresses fairness-accuracy tradeoff

entries.title: Evaluation of performance measures in predictive artificial intelligence models to support medical decisions: overview and guidance
key_requirements: Classic and contemporary performance measures assessed
Focus on models estimating probabilities for binary outcomes
Calibration alongside discrimination is essential
Multi-metric evaluation framework for medical AI

entries.title: Regulation (EU) 2024/1689 — Artificial Intelligence Act
entries.journal: Official Journal of the European Union
key_requirements: Risk-based classification of AI systems
High-risk AI (including medical devices) requires conformity assessment
Transparency obligations for AI providers
Data governance and bias prevention requirements
Post-market monitoring obligations

agent_instructions.on_query: Search entries by: (1) category for topic-specific literature, (2) dimensions_affected for scoring-related literature, (3) gates_implementing for gate-justification literature, (4) journal for journal-specific methodology
agent_instructions.on_new_finding: Append entry with next sequential LIT-NNN ID. Verify: (1) DOI is valid, (2) Journal meets quality criteria, (3) key_requirements extracted accurately, (4) gates_implementing mapped correctly
agent_instructions.on_update: If a guideline is updated (e.g., TRIPOD 2015 → TRIPOD+AI 2024), mark the old entry with 'superseded_by' field pointing to new entry ID
agent_instructions.quality_gate: Only include: (1) Published in IF>10 journal OR domain-leading specialty, (2) Guidelines from EQUATOR/Cochrane/FDA/MHRA/EMA, (3) Foundational methodology papers (>500 citations), (4) Systematic reviews following PRISMA
agent_instructions.id_format: LIT-NNN where NNN is zero-padded sequential number
